multiplechoiceresponse.label: The root node of the tree splits on thal_2 ≤ 0.5. Which statement best reflects what this split tells you about the model’s reasoning?

choicegroup.choice: The model first checks thal_2 because it alone determines the final prediction.
The model uses thal_2 to separate patients into two groups that will be evaluated using different subsequent rules.
Patients with thal_2 > 0.5 are always predicted to have disease.
thal_2 is the only feature needed to explain all predictions.

div.p: In a decision tree, each split determines which rules will be applied next. The root split on thal_2 does not finalize the prediction; it routes patients into two subgroups that are then evaluated using different features and thresholds. This highlights how trees create conditional explanations rather than global ones.
